conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point (CAP) for PPO block
conditions.evidence_text: As can be seen in Fig. 4, PPGs elute sufficiently close to critical conditions at a range between 81 and 85 wt% THF. In such a mobile phase, a separation of the block copolymer from PPGs and PPOs with allylic end group can be achieved (Fig. 5). ... LCCC of a diblock copolymer and the corresponding homopolymers on Prodigy ODS3 in 84.99 wt% THF (CAP for PPO); detection: ELSD.
conditions.notes: Analytical scale LCCC used to separate block copolymer from homopolymers based on PPO critical conditions.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point (CAP) for PPO block
conditions.evidence_text: A similar separation is also possible on a semipreparative column: even with a rather large sample loop, the individual peaks are sufficiently resolved (Fig. 6). The fraction limits are indicated by dotted lines. ... LCCC of a diblock copolymer (MeO-PEG 2000-b-PPO) on Spherisorb ODS2 (semipreparative) in 81.14 wt% THF (CAP of PPO); detection: density.
conditions.notes: Semipreparative scale LCCC used for fractionation of the diblock copolymer. The text explicitly identifies this as LCCC at CAP for PPO.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria
